2604.08281.title: When to Trust Tools? Adaptive Tool Trust Calibration For Tool-Integrated Math Reasoning
2604.08281.paper_summary: Large reasoning models (LRMs) have achieved strong performance enhancement through scaling test time computation, but due to the inherent limitations of the underlying language models, they still have shortcomings in tasks that require precise computation and extensive knowledge reserves. Tool-Integrated Reasoning (TIR) has emerged as a promising paradigm that incorporates tool call and execution within the reasoning trajectory. Although recent works have released some powerful open-source TIR models, our analysis reveals that these models still suffer from critical deficiencies. We find that when the reasoning of the model conflicts with the tool results, the model tends to believe in its own reasoning. And there are cases where the tool results are correct but are ignored by the model, resulting in incorrect answers, which we define as "Tool Ignored''. This indicates that the model does not know when to trust or ignore the tool. To overcome these limitations, We introduce Adaptive Tool Trust Calibration (ATTC), a novel framework that guides the model to adaptively choose to trust or ignore the tool results based on the confidence score of generated code blocks. The experimental results from various open-source TIR models of different sizes and across multiple datasets demonstrate that ATTC effectively reduces the "Tool Ignored" issue, resulting in a performance increase of 4.1% to 7.5%.
2604.08281.paper_summary_zh: 大型推理模型（LRMs）透過擴大測試時間計算實現了強大的性能增強，但由於底層語言模型的固有限制，它們在需要精確計算和廣泛知識儲備的任務中仍然存在不足。工具整合推理（TIR）作為一種有前景的範式出現，將工具調用和執行納入推理過程中。儘管最近的研究推出了一些強大的開源TIR模型，我們的分析顯示這些模型仍然存在關鍵缺陷。我們發現當模型的推理與工具結果相衝突時，模型傾向於相信自己的推理。而且有些情況下，工具結果是正確的，但被模型忽略，導致錯誤答案，我們將其定義為「工具被忽略」。這表明模型不知道何時應該信任或忽略工具。為了克服這些限制，我們引入了自適應工具信任校準（ATTC），這是一個新穎的框架，指導模型根據生成代碼塊的置信度分數自適應地選擇信任或忽略工具結果。來自不同大小的各種開源TIR模型和多個數據集的實驗結果表明，ATTC有效減少了「工具被忽略」的問題，性能提升幅度為4.1%到7.5%。
2604.08281.author: Ruotao Xu et.al.
2604.08281.authors: Ruotao Xu, Yixin Ji, Yu Luo, Jinpeng Li, Dong Li, Peifeng Li, Juntao Li, Min Zhang
